evidence_spans.span: A statistical model is a family of probability distributions indexed by an unknown parameter \(\theta\).

The score function is the derivative of the log-likelihood with respect to the parameter,

\[
s(\theta) = \nabla_{\theta}\ell(\theta).
\]

results.rationale: The Score Function Formula explicitly defines the score function as the derivative of the log-likelihood, which is derived from the statistical model. The formula formalizes the concept of the score function within the context of the statistical model.
